meta.title: {{appName}} Pricing
meta.description: Choose the {{appName}} plan that fits your VRChat avatar workflow. Subscribe for monthly credits or buy credit packs when you need more generations.

page.title: {{appName}} pricing for every stage of avatar creation
page.description: Choose a subscription for ongoing avatar work and add extra credit packs whenever you need more generations, textures, or rigging jobs.
page.trustText: Simple pricing for creating, texturing, and rigging VRChat avatars with {{appName}}

unsureSection.title: Not sure which {{appName}} plan fits you?
unsureSection.description: Choose Basic if you are starting out with avatar creation, Standard if you build avatars regularly, Pro for high-volume creation, and Max for studio-level production needs.
unsureSection.linkText: Talk to us about the right plan

faq.title: {{appName}} pricing questions
faq.description: Everything you need to know about credits, plans, billing, and how much avatar work each option can support.

items.question: How do {{appName}} credits work?
items.answer: Credits are used when you run generation and asset workflow tasks in {{appName}}. Different actions cost different amounts depending on the workload. Text to Image uses 5 credits, while more advanced actions like 3D generation and auto rigging use more.

items.question: What do common actions cost?
items.answer: Current usage is simple: Text to 3D costs 10 credits without texture or 20 with texture, Image to 3D costs 20 or 30, Multiview to 3D costs 20 or 30, Text to Image costs 5, and Auto Rigging costs 35 credits.

items.question: How much can I create with a plan?
items.answer: It depends on your workflow. For example, 800 credits can cover around 160 Text to Image generations, 40 Text to 3D jobs with texture, or a mixed workflow across concept, model, texture, and rigging. Higher plans are better if you create multiple variants or refine assets often.

items.question: What changes between the plans?
items.answer: The main difference is how many credits you get each month and the generation speed. Basic is for getting started, Standard is for regular avatar creation, Pro is for power users, and Max is for studio-level production.

items.question: Can I buy extra credits without upgrading my plan?
items.answer: Yes. Credit packs are available separately, so you can stay on your current subscription and add more credits whenever you need extra generations, textures, or rigging runs.

items.question: Is there a way to try {{appName}} before paying?
items.answer: Yes. New users receive starter credits upon signup so you can test the core avatar workflow before committing to a subscription.

items.question: What happens to unused subscription credits?
items.answer: Unused subscription credits are best treated as monthly usage credits for active workflows. You can explain the exact rollover policy in product docs or billing settings, but this pricing page should position them as tied to the current billing period.

items.question: Do credit packs expire?
items.answer: Credit packs work best as flexible top-ups for extra usage. You can define the final expiration policy in billing, but they should be presented here as a way to extend usage without changing plans.

items.question: How do I manage or cancel my subscription?
items.answer: You can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel your {{appName}} subscription from your billing settings. Any plan changes apply to future billing cycles based on your payment provider setup.
